Title: Testing a chemical series inspired by plant stress oxylipin signalling agents for herbicide safening activity

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly inducible by fenclorim and selected oxylipin-inspired electrophilic compounds in cell culture
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured after 1 h chemical exposure in Arabidopsis cell cultures
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification of xenobiotics and association with herbicide safening responses
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 100 μM fenclorim; oxylipin-inspired compound treatments; comparison with OPDA and PPA1 transcriptomic responses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript used as a rapid xenobiotic response biomarker; induced by compounds 11, 12, 15, 16, 17, 19 and 20, with compound 12 exceeding fenclorim for induction
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in Arabidopsis cell cultures after 60 min exposure; comparative transcriptomics/microarray correlation analyses
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Induction correlated only weakly with rice protection and did not confer practical safening by itself
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray studies in GENEVESTIGATOR: fenclorim experiment AT-00459; PPA1 experiment AT-00293; OPDA experiment AT-00293
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful biomarker for screening compounds that trigger rapid xenobiotic response rather than a direct engineering target in this paper
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGSTU19 served as a rapid xenobiotic-response marker in Arabidopsis. 2. Its transcript was significantly induced by several oxylipin-inspired electrophilic compounds, especially compound 12. 3. Stronger induction generally paralleled weak protection trends in rice seedlings, supporting linkage between xenobiotic-response activation and safener-like activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a marker gene rather than manipulated genetically; no direct metabolite product quantified.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly inducible marker of safener response, but less responsive to the test series than to fenclorim
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Measured after 1 h chemical exposure in Arabidopsis cell cultures
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification-associated marker linked to safener-induced protective metabolism
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript used as a rapid xenobiotic response biomarker; significantly induced by compounds 12, 19 and 20, but fenclorim remained much more effective
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR in Arabidopsis cell cultures after 60 min exposure
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Marker induction did not translate into significant functional safening in rice under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray comparison framework described via GENEVESTIGATOR datasets
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful diagnostic biomarker for rapid safener-like signaling screens
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGSTU24 was used as a second rapid xenobiotic-response marker. 2. Only compounds 12, 19 and 20 significantly induced its transcript. 3. It was less responsive to the synthetic series than to fenclorim, indicating stricter structural requirements for activation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used only as biomarker; no genetic perturbation of AtGSTU24 reported.
